ratings.comment: She is very knowledgeable about her field; however, her the coursework is very repetitive. She reuses slides from entry level courses in grad school courses and assigns exact assignments completed during class as homework questions. Overall, she is a passionate professor, but most of her coursework is very repetitive busy work.

Nice professor but can be very passive aggressive at times. Attendance is mandatory for discussion but not for lecture. Weekly homework assignments worth 15 points. Fair grading in regards to projects, homework, and exams. Would take again. Does not require you to buy a text book which is nice.

Very very hard to fail this class. Come to class and do the group assignments (very easy). Theres only like 3 tests and 4 reading assignments. By far my easiest class. Make sure to take this class in a tough semester.

Great professor who is very understanding of students. Genuinely cares that you learn course material and aren't just stressing over grade aspect. Hw/In-class participation activities had 70% worth of overall grade, making exams not as stressful, no cumulative exams as well.

Great professor and plenty of office hours if needed. Textbook wasn't required, but definitely recommend because she reads from it word for word in lecture. Attendance matters for certain in class assignment days. Tests were honestly kind of hard, but if you make a good note card, not too bad. Focuses on your understanding over stressing grades.

Took this class as a psych major but it was super interesting and I learned a lot. Prof. Silber is super caring and very passionate about the subject matter. However, in her lectures she speaks very fast with no time for questions and she will say quick anecdotes or examples that will later be on the exam. Wish I would have bought the textbook.

This class is not very hard if you have interest in design related topics. The issue I had with this class was silber's overuse of plane examples. She explains everything using complicated plane examples and I found myself having to look things up about planes just so I could understand her examples. Apart from that she's a pretty chill professor.

I had Hannah for two classes this semester and really enjoyed both. She wants to see her students succeed and all her assignments and exams are very fair. If you put the effort in it isn't too hard to do well.

Very helpful, understanding, and accommodating! She's more than willing to further explain concepts, and does seem genuinely interested in the topic. Understands the woman in stem struggle. Near the end of the semester attendance dropped & she got v passive-aggressive about it and many lectures were cut short, but that is my only complaint.

Absolute gem of a Professor and person. As a busy masters student, Dr. Silber is a breath of fresh air w/a focus on what matters and building relationships with students. Her lectures are flexible, online & in person; she is incredibly considerate to the fact we all have busy lives. If you come to class and converse, you'll have a good time.
